title: SQL Analysis & Reporting
subtitle: Turn single-table data into calculated, grouped, and decision-ready business reports.
description: A cumulative single-table SQL reporting course covering aliases, calculations, NULL safety, aggregate functions, GROUP BY, HAVING, CASE, conditional aggregation, module projects, and a final executive-report capstone.
audience: Learners who completed SQL Foundations / SQL V2.
Beginners who can already use SELECT, WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT, LIKE, IN, BETWEEN, and NULL filters.
Learners preparing for practical reporting, analytics, and business-intelligence work.
prerequisites: Read a single database table and identify rows and columns.
Select specific columns and use readable filters.
Combine conditions with AND, OR, and NOT.
Sort and limit results and work with LIKE, IN, BETWEEN, and IS NULL.
goals: Create readable result columns with aliases, calculations, and rounding.
Handle missing values deliberately with COALESCE.
Build one-row KPI snapshots with aggregate functions.
Build grouped scorecards with GROUP BY and deterministic sorting.
Filter grouped reports with HAVING and calculate conditional KPIs with CASE.
Complete five cumulative module projects and one final executive-report capstone.

notes: Every technical sketch must receive a required embedded Try It code_input with id/exerciseKey formatted as try-<topic-id>-sketchN.
Conceptual-only topics may omit Try It, but quizzes remain non-codeInput.
Every standard module must contain exactly one role=module_project section with one progressive project topic.
The final module must contain exactly one role=capstone section and no ordinary module project section.
Module projects and capstone steps are progressive: step N+1 starterCode must equal step N full solutionCode.
Lesson examples, Try It prompts, module projects, and the capstone must use different reporting questions, grouping targets, conditions, or thresholds.
All graded SQL must produce deterministic expected columns, rows, values, and order.
Use only the sales_kpi dataset and its sales_reporting table. Do not introduce joins, subqueries, CTEs, window functions, or data mutation.
